questions.question: Explain the concept of 'Requirements Traceability' and describe how it helps in maintaining consistency between the requirements model and the design model in software engineering. Also, mention the two types of traceability matrices used in practice.
keyPoints: Requirements traceability links each requirement to design components and code
Forward traceability maps requirements to design elements and test cases
Backward traceability ensures all design elements trace back to original requirements
Helps identify missing implementations and validate complete coverage

questions.question: Consider an online banking system where a customer can transfer funds between accounts. Draw a UML sequence diagram showing the interaction between Customer, TransferInterface, AccountManager, and BankDatabase. Include appropriate message exchanges for validation, debit, credit operations, and handle a case where insufficient balance exists.
keyPoints: Correct identification of actors and objects with lifelines
Use of combined fragment for conditional balance check logic
Return messages for success and failure scenarios

questions.question: A hospital management system requires the following functional requirements: Patient registration and appointment scheduling, Medical records management, Doctor's prescription and diagnostic reports, Billing and insurance claim processing, and Inventory management for pharmacy. As a software engineer, develop a comprehensive design model by: a) Proposing an appropriate architectural style with justification and identify the main subsystems. b) Applying the Factory Method and Observer design patterns where applicable, with class diagram snippets showing key classes and their relationships. c) Drawing a high-level component diagram showing all major subsystems and their dependencies. d) Explain how each functional requirement maps to specific design components.
keyPoints: Layered or MVC architecture with presentation, business logic, and data layers
Factory Method for creating different types of medical records; Observer for patient status updates to multiple departments
Component diagram with clear dependencies between subsystems like Registration, Records, Billing, Pharmacy
Requirements-to-component mapping showing which subsystem handles each requirement

questions.question: Explain the fundamental differences between 'Requirements Model' and 'Design Model' in software engineering. Also, elaborate on the process of transforming an object-oriented requirements model into a design model by addressing: a) How use case analysis leads to the identification of analysis classes (Entity, Boundary, Control classes). b) The transformation of analysis classes into design classes with detailed responsibilities. c) The role of design patterns in refining the design model, citing at least two patterns with practical examples from an Inventory Management System.
keyPoints: Requirements model captures 'what' the system must do; Design model captures 'how' it will be implemented
Use cases → Analysis classes: Entity (data), Boundary (interfaces), Control (business logic) separation
Design classes add implementation details, methods, visibility, relationships, and persistence attributes
Singleton pattern for inventory manager, Strategy pattern for discount calculation, Repository pattern for data access
